findings.title: SAST: Other (10 instances)
findings.description: Semgrep static analysis detected 10 ERROR-severity other issues in /home/ss/code/juice-shop. Example: Detected a sequelize statement that is tainted by user-input. This could lead to SQL injection if the variable is user-controlled and is not properly sanitized. In order to prevent SQL injection, it is recommended to use parameterized queries or prepared statements.
cwe_ids: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

findings.title: SAST: Auth (3 instances)
findings.description: Semgrep static analysis detected 3 ERROR-severity auth issues in /home/ss/code/juice-shop. Example: JWT token detected
cwe_ids: CWE-321: Use of Hard-coded Cryptographic Key

findings.title: Hardcoded Secrets in Source Code (148 detected)
findings.description: Secret scanning tools found 148 credentials embedded in /home/ss/code/juice-shop (145 by Gitleaks, 3 by TruffleHog). Exposed secrets enable direct unauthorized access to connected services.

findings.title: Outdated jQuery Version (CVE-2015-9251)
findings.remediation: Upgrade jQuery to version 3.5.0 or later. Audit code for usage of `.load()` with dynamic parameters.

metadata.impact: Remote Code Execution (XSS), session hijacking, credential theft, and defacement.
metadata.exploitability: Moderate — Requires the application to use the vulnerable `.load()` method with user input, which is common in older jQuery apps.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: false_positive_indicators; Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: Verbose Error Messages Exposing Internal Logic
findings.evidence: `"title":"Error: Blocked illegal activity by ::ffff:192.168.1.144"`, `"title":"SyntaxError: Unexpected token : in JSON at position 0"`, `"title":"TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'includes')"`
findings.remediation: Configure the application to return generic error pages (e.g., "500 Internal Server Error") without exposing stack traces or internal details.

metadata.impact: Information leakage, aiding in attack surface mapping and bypassing security controls.
metadata.exploitability: Easy — Errors are triggered by malformed requests.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: evidence_quality, false_positive_indicators; Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: Overly Permissive CORS Configuration
findings.remediation: Configure the web server to restrict `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` to a specific list of trusted domains (e.g., `https://trusted-app.com`). If cross-origin access is not required, remove the header entirely to enforce the Same-Origin Policy.

metadata.impact: Data exfiltration of unauthenticated API responses (e.g., user lists, product catalogs) via Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) or direct browser-based scraping.
metadata.exploitability: Moderate — Requires the attacker to host a malicious page and trick a victim into visiting it. Impact is limited to unauthenticated data exposure.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: evidence_quality, tool_reliability, false_positive_indicators; High overall confidence

findings.title: Missing Content Security Policy (CSP)
findings.evidence: Alerts for "Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set" on `http://192.168.1.49:3000`, `http://192.168.1.49:3000/api/Users`, and `http://192.168.1.49:3000/file-upload`.
findings.remediation: Implement a strict Content Security Policy header. Start with a restrictive policy (e.g., `default-src 'self'`) and gradually add necessary sources (e.g., `script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com`).

metadata.impact: Increased risk of successful Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks, potentially leading to session hijacking, defacement, or malware distribution.
metadata.exploitability: Low — This is a missing defense mechanism. It does not directly allow an attack but significantly lowers the bar for successful XSS exploitation.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: evidence_quality, tool_reliability, false_positive_indicators; Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: Inclusion of Third-Party JavaScript
findings.remediation: Verify the necessity of the external script. If required, implement Subresource Integrity (SRI) by adding an `integrity` attribute to the script tag to ensure the file has not been tampered with.
owasp_categories: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures

metadata.impact: Potential for supply chain attacks, leading to XSS or data theft if the external script is modified.
metadata.exploitability: Low — Requires a compromise of the third-party CDN or the specific library version.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: evidence_quality, tool_reliability, false_positive_indicators; Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: Tooling Configuration Errors (Non-Vulnerability)
findings.evidence: `"Failed to disable robots.txt handling: ... HTTP Error 400: Bad Request"` and `"AJAX spider failed: ... HTTP Error 400: Bad Request"`.
findings.remediation: Check ZAP API configuration, ensure the API key is provided if required, and verify network connectivity between the scanner and the target.

metadata.impact: None on the target; may result in incomplete scanning coverage.
metadata.exploitability: N/A
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: tool_reliability; Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: Identification of Known Vulnerable Application (OWASP Juice Shop)
findings.evidence: `<title>OWASP Juice Shop</title>` and `<meta name="description" content="Probably the most modern and sophisticated insecure web application">`
findings.remediation:
1. **Immediate Action:** If this is a production system, take it offline immediately.
2. **Verification:** Confirm if this is an authorized training environment.
3. **Removal:** Do not deploy OWASP Juice Shop in any production or internet-facing environment.
4. **Network Segmentation:** If used for training, ensure it is strictly isolated from the corporate network and internet.

metadata.impact: Complete compromise of the application, unauthorized access to the database, user account takeover, and potential server-side code execution.
metadata.exploitability: Easy — The application is designed to be vulnerable. Attackers can immediately exploit known, documented flaws without needing to perform discovery or fuzzing.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: tool_reliability, context_relevance, false_positive_indicators; High overall confidence

findings.title: Overly Permissive Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS)
findings.remediation:
1. Replace `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` with specific, trusted origins (e.g., `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://trusted-domain.com`).
2. Implement strict CSRF tokens for state-changing operations.
3. Review the `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` and `Access-Control-Allow-Headers` to ensure they are not overly permissive.

metadata.impact: Potential for data exfiltration or unauthorized state-changing requests if the application relies on cookies for authentication without proper CSRF tokens.
metadata.exploitability: Moderate — Requires a victim to be logged into the application while visiting a malicious site.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: tool_reliability, false_positive_indicators; Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: Information Disclosure via Service Banner
findings.remediation: Configure nginx to suppress version numbers in server headers (`server_tokens off;`).

metadata.impact: Reduces the effort required for an attacker to tailor an exploit.
metadata.exploitability: Hard — Requires finding a specific vulnerability for nginx 1.24.0.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: tool_reliability; Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: Insecure CORS Configuration (Wildcard Origin)
findings.remediation: Replace the wildcard `*` with specific, trusted domain names (e.g., `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://trusted-domain.com`). If cross-origin access is not required, remove the header entirely.

metadata.impact: Potential data exfiltration of sensitive information returned in the response body; facilitates Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks if the attacker can inject scripts that read the response.
metadata.exploitability: Easy — An attacker only needs to host a malicious page that loads the target resource.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: false_positive_indicators; Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: Information Disclosure via Custom Header
findings.remediation: Remove the `X-Recruiting` header from the HTTP response unless it is strictly necessary for public-facing functionality.

metadata.impact: Assists attackers in reconnaissance, potentially leading to the discovery of unauthenticated endpoints or internal tools.
metadata.exploitability: Easy — The information is passively disclosed in every HTTP response.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: false_positive_indicators; Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: Sensitive Path Exposure
findings.description: The path `/assets/private/` is visible. While the name "private" suggests restricted access, the tool successfully enumerated these files. This indicates the server is serving them publicly. However, without a 403/401 error or a specific tool flag indicating "unauthorized access," this is an information disclosure of asset paths, not necessarily a confirmed vulnerability unless the content is sensitive (which we cannot verify from the filename alone).

metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: verification_status; Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: jQuery 2.2.4 End-of-Life and Multiple XSS Vulnerabilities
findings.remediation: Upgrade jQuery to the latest stable version (3.7.1 or higher) immediately. If the application does not require jQuery, remove the library entirely. Ensure that any input passed to jQuery DOM methods is properly sanitized or encoded.

metadata.impact: Successful exploitation allows attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript in the victim's browser (XSS), leading to session hijacking, credential theft, or defacement. Object prototype pollution could lead to Denial of Service or logic manipulation.
metadata.exploitability: Moderate — Requires the application to use jQuery's DOM manipulation methods (`.html()`, `.append()`, `.parseHTML()`) on untrusted input. If the application sanitizes input before passing it to jQuery, the risk is mitigated, but the library version itself remains a vector.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: evidence_quality, context_relevance, false_positive_indicators; Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: jQuery 2.2.4 End-of-Life Status
findings.evidence: `"summary":"jQuery 1.x and 2.x are End-of-Life and no longer receiving security updates","retid":"73","issue":"162","severity":"low"`
findings.remediation: Upgrade to a supported version of jQuery.

metadata.impact: Increased attack surface due to lack of future security updates.
metadata.exploitability: N/A (Precondition for other vulnerabilities).
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: false_positive_indicators; Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: Information Disclosure via Metrics Endpoint
findings.remediation:
1. Restrict access to the `/metrics` endpoint to localhost (127.0.0.1) or a specific internal network range.
2. Implement authentication (e.g., Basic Auth, API Keys) for the endpoint.
3. Review the content of the metrics output to ensure no sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII) is inadvertently included.

metadata.impact: Information leakage including application version, internal IP addresses, JVM/Node.js statistics, and uptime. This data aids attackers in crafting targeted exploits (e.g., finding CVEs for specific versions) or understanding the system architecture.
metadata.exploitability: Easy — The endpoint is publicly accessible via a simple HTTP GET request.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: false_positive_indicators; Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: Potential Error Handling Issues on Sensitive Endpoints
findings.remediation:
1. Configure the web server/application to return generic 500 error pages to unauthenticated users.
2. Ensure logging captures the error details server-side, but never expose them to the client.
3. Verify that these endpoints require valid authentication tokens or parameters before processing.

metadata.impact: If the 500 response body contains stack traces, it reveals internal logic, file paths, and library versions, aiding attackers.
metadata.exploitability: Moderate — Requires inspecting the response body of the 500 error to confirm if sensitive data is leaked.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: Critical Exposure of Sensitive Backup and Configuration Files
findings.evidence:
"+ /192.168.1.49.jks: Potentially interesting backup/cert file found. . See: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/530.html"
"+ /database.war: Potentially interesting backup/cert file found. . See: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/530.html"
"+ /backup.tar: Potentially interesting backup/cert file found. . See: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/530.html"
"+ /192.168.1.49.pem: Potentially interesting backup/cert file found. . See: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/530.html"
findings.remediation:
1. Immediately remove all sensitive files (`.jks`, `.war`, `.tar`, `.pem`, `.bak`, etc.) from the web-accessible directory.
2. Configure the web server (Nginx/Apache) to deny access to these file extensions: `location ~ \.(jks|war|tar|pem|egg|cer|alz)$ { deny all; }`.
3. Ensure backups are stored outside the web root or in a directory protected by authentication.
4. Rotate all credentials and private keys found in these files immediately, assuming they are compromised.

metadata.impact: Full compromise of the application. Attackers can extract private keys (`.jks`, `.pem`), download database dumps (`.tar`, `.war`), and analyze source code for further vulnerabilities. This leads to data breach, credential theft, and potential server takeover.
metadata.exploitability: Easy — Files are publicly accessible via direct URL requests without authentication.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: evidence_quality, tool_reliability, context_relevance; High overall confidence

findings.title: Insecure CORS Configuration (Wildcard Origin)
findings.remediation: Restrict the `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header to specific trusted domains (e.g., `https://yourdomain.com`) instead of using the wildcard `*`.

metadata.impact: Potential for data exfiltration or unauthorized actions performed on behalf of the user.
metadata.exploitability: Moderate — Requires a victim to be logged in and visit a malicious site.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: tool_reliability, false_positive_indicators; Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: Information Disclosure via Robots.txt
findings.evidence: "+ /robots.txt: Entry '/ftp/' is returned a non-forbidden or redirect HTTP code (200)."
findings.remediation: Review the `/ftp/` directory. If it contains sensitive data, restrict access. If it is not needed, remove it. Update `robots.txt` to disallow crawling of sensitive paths.

metadata.impact: Aids attackers in reconnaissance by revealing hidden or sensitive directories.
metadata.exploitability: Easy — Path is confirmed to exist.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: tool_reliability; Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: Missing X-Content-Type-Options Header
findings.evidence: "+ assets/public/favicon_js.ico: The X-Content-Type-Options header is not set."
findings.remediation: Add the header `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` to all HTTP responses.

metadata.impact: Potential for Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) if the browser misinterprets a file type.
metadata.exploitability: Moderate — Requires specific browser behavior and content type confusion.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: tool_reliability, false_positive_indicators; Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: Exposed Sensitive Backup and Configuration Files
findings.evidence: `+ /site.tar.bz2: Potentially interesting backup/cert file found. . See: https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/530.html` (and 60+ similar entries including `/database.jks`, `/192.168.1.49.war`, `/dump.pem`)
findings.remediation: Immediately remove all backup files, archives, and sensitive configuration files from the web-accessible directory. Configure the web server (Nginx/Apache) to deny access to files with extensions `.tar`, `.gz`, `.war`, `.jks`, `.pem`, `.egg`, `.alz`, `.cer`.

metadata.impact: Full disclosure of source code, database contents, and private keys. This can lead to complete system compromise, credential theft, and data breach.
metadata.exploitability: Easy — An attacker can simply download these files via a browser or `curl` without authentication.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: evidence_quality, tool_reliability, context_relevance; High overall confidence

findings.title: Local File Inclusion (LFI) in NextGEN Gallery Plugin
findings.remediation: Update the NextGEN Gallery plugin to the latest version or remove it if not in use. Ensure the server is patched against the specific CVE associated with this LFI.

metadata.impact: Attackers can read sensitive files (e.g., `/etc/passwd`, `.htpasswd`, source code). In some configurations, this can be chained to Remote Code Execution (RCE) if the attacker can include a remote file or write to a log file.
metadata.exploitability: Moderate — Requires the specific plugin version to be present and the path to be accessible. The output confirms the file exists.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: evidence_quality, tool_reliability, context_relevance, false_positive_indicators; High overall confidence

findings.title: Insecure CORS Configuration
findings.remediation: Configure the server to set `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` to a specific trusted domain (e.g., `https://example.com`) instead of `*`.

metadata.impact: Potential for data theft if the application has other vulnerabilities (e.g., missing CSRF tokens) or if sensitive data is returned in the response.
metadata.exploitability: Easy — No special skills required; any malicious site can trigger this.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: tool_reliability, false_positive_indicators; Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: Missing X-Content-Type-Options Header
findings.evidence: `+ assets/public/favicon_js.ico: The X-Content-Type-Options header is not set.`
findings.remediation: Configure the web server to include the header `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` in all responses.

metadata.impact: Potential for Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) if the browser misinterprets the content type.
metadata.exploitability: Moderate — Requires the attacker to host a malicious file that the server serves with an incorrect MIME type.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: tool_reliability, false_positive_indicators; Medium confidence, may need verification

findings.title: Information Disclosure via Robots.txt
findings.evidence: `+ /robots.txt: Entry '/ftp/' is returned a non-forbidden or redirect HTTP code (200).`
findings.remediation: Review the contents of `/ftp/`. If it contains sensitive data, restrict access via server configuration. Remove the entry from `robots.txt` if the directory should not be indexed or discovered.

metadata.impact: Reveals the existence of a potentially sensitive directory (`/ftp/`) which might contain files not intended for public viewing.
metadata.exploitability: Easy — The path is publicly disclosed.
metadata.confidence_reasoning: Strong: tool_reliability; Medium confidence, may need verification

ai_decisions.decision:
### REASONING

**Step 1 — Parse:**
I analyzed the `httpx` output for `http://192.168.1.49:3000`.
- **Target Identification:** The root path `/` returns title "OWASP Juice Shop" (a known vulnerable application for training).
- **Technology Stack:** Detected `jQuery:2.2.4`, `Cloudflare`, `Onsen UI`, `Osano:3.1.0`, `cdnjs`.
- **Endpoints & Status Codes:**
  - `200 OK`: `/metrics`, `/api/Recycles`, `/rest/chatbot/status`, `/rest/admin/application-version`, `/rest/admin/application-configuration`, `/rest/user/whoami`, `/rest/track-order/:id`, `/rest/repeat-notification`, `/rest/products/:id/reviews`.
  - `401 Unauthorized`: Multiple API endpoints (`/api/Complaints`, `/api/Users`, `/api/BasketItems`, etc.) returning "UnauthorizedError: No Authorization header was found".
  - `403 Forbidden`: `/api/Quantitys/:id`, `/rest/order-history/orders`.
  - `500 Internal Server Error`: Multiple endpoints returning stack traces or specific error messages (e.g., "Unexpected path", "SyntaxError", "TypeError", "Blocked illegal activity").
- **Specific Findings:**
  - **Info Disclosure:** `/metrics` (Prometheus metrics) and `/rest/admin/application-configuration` (large JSON config) are accessible without authentication.
  - **Version Disclosure:** `jQuery:2.2.4` is detected. This version is known to have XSS vulnerabilities (CVE-2015-9251).
  - **Error Handling:** Several 500 errors expose internal logic (e.g., "Blocked illegal activity by ::ffff:192.168.1.144"), indicating the application is in a debug or verbose error state, or the scanner triggered a WAF/Rate limiter.
  - **Authentication:** Most sensitive endpoints correctly return 401, suggesting authentication is enforced, though the lack of auth on `/metrics` and `/admin/application-configuration` is a misconfiguration.

**Step 2 — Pattern:**
- **CVE-2015-9251 (jQuery XSS):** The detection of `jQuery:2.2.4` is a strong indicator. This version is vulnerable to Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) via the `.load()` method.
- **Information Disclosure:** The `/metrics` endpoint exposes internal performance data. The `/rest/admin/application-configuration` endpoint exposes application settings, potentially including secrets or internal paths.
- **Verbose Error Messages:** The 500 errors reveal internal paths and logic, aiding attackers in mapping the application structure.

**Step 3 — Validate:**
- **jQuery:** Explicitly listed in `tech` array: `"jQuery:2.2.4"`.
- **Metrics:** `status_code":200` for `/metrics`.
- **Config:** `status_code":200` for `/rest/admin/application-configuration` with `content_length":18843`.
- **Auth Bypass:** The 401s on `/api/Users` confirm auth is required there, but the 200s on `/rest/admin/application-configuration` suggest a lack of auth on that specific admin endpoint.

**Step 4 — Assess:**
- **jQuery XSS:** High severity. Exploitable if the application uses `.load()` with user input.
- **Info Disclosure (Config/Metrics):** Medium severity. Leaks internal topology and configuration.
- **Verbose Errors:** Low/Medium. Aids reconnaissance.

**Step 5 — Impact:**
- **XSS:** Attacker can execute arbitrary JS in the context of the victim's browser, stealing cookies/sessions.
- **Info Disclosure:** Attacker gains knowledge of internal services, versions, and configuration parameters, facilitating further attacks.

**Step 6 — Remediate:**
- Upgrade jQuery.
- Restrict access to `/metrics` and `/rest/admin/*` endpoints.
- Implement generic error pages.

### RESPONSE

### FINDING: Outdated jQuery Version (CVE-2015-9251)
SEVERITY: High
EVIDENCE: `"tech":["Cloudflare","Onsen UI","Osano:3.1.0","cdnjs","jQuery:2.2.4"]`
REASONING: The output explicitly identifies `jQuery:2.2.4`. This version is vulnerable to Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) via the `.load()` method (CVE-2015-9251). If the application uses this method to load content from user-controlled URLs, an attacker can inject malicious scripts.
EXPLOITABILITY: Moderate — Requires the application to use the vulnerable `.load()` method with user input, which is common in older jQuery apps.
IMPACT: Remote Code Execution (XSS), session hijacking, credential theft, and defacement.
REMEDIATION: Upgrade jQuery to version 3.5.0 or later. Audit code for usage of `.load()` with dynamic parameters.
CVSS: 6.1 (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N)
CWE: CWE-79 (Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation)
OWASP: A03:2021 - Injection (XSS)

### FINDING: Sensitive Information Disclosure via Admin Configuration Endpoint
SEVERITY: Medium
EVIDENCE: `"url":"http://192.168.1.49:3000/rest/admin/application-configuration","status_code":200,"content_length":18843`
REASONING: The endpoint `/rest/admin/application-configuration` returns a 200 OK status with a large payload (18KB) without requiring authentication. This endpoint likely exposes internal configuration, environment variables, or service endpoints, which aids attackers in mapping the application architecture.
EXPLOITABILITY: Easy — The endpoint is publicly accessible via a simple GET request.
IMPACT: Information leakage, exposure of internal infrastructure details, potential discovery of secrets or API keys.
REMEDIATION: Implement authentication and authorization checks on all `/rest/admin/*` endpoints. Restrict access to internal networks or specific IP ranges if necessary.
CVSS: 5.3 (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N)
CWE: CWE-200 (Information Exposure)
OWASP: A05:2021 - Security Misconfiguration

### FINDING: Prometheus Metrics Endpoint Exposed
SEVERITY: Low
EVIDENCE: `"url":"http://192.168.1.49:3000/metrics","status_code":200,"content_type":"text/plain"`
REASONING: The `/metrics` endpoint is accessible without authentication. While not directly exploitable for code execution, it exposes detailed performance metrics, request counts, and potentially internal service names, which can be used for reconnaissance.
EXPLOITABILITY: Easy — Publicly accessible.
IMPACT: Reconnaissance, understanding application load and behavior, potential identification of internal service names.
REMEDIATION: Restrict access to `/metrics` to internal monitoring systems only (e.g., via firewall rules or authentication middleware).
CVSS: 3.7 (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N)
CWE: CWE-200 (Information Exposure)
OWASP: A05:2021 - Security Misconfiguration

### FINDING: Verbose Error Messages Exposing Internal Logic
SEVERITY: Low
EVIDENCE: `"title":"Error: Blocked illegal activity by ::ffff:192.168.1.144"`, `"title":"SyntaxError: Unexpected token : in JSON at position 0"`, `"title":"TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'includes')"`
REASONING: Multiple endpoints return 500 errors with detailed stack traces or specific error messages (e.g., "Blocked illegal activity", "Unexpected path"). This reveals internal logic, variable names, and the presence of security controls (like rate limiting or WAF), aiding attackers in crafting targeted attacks.
EXPLOITABILITY: Easy — Errors are triggered by malformed requests.
IMPACT: Information leakage, aiding in attack surface mapping and bypassing security controls.
REMEDIATION: Configure the application to return generic error pages (e.g., "500 Internal Server Error") without exposing stack traces or internal details.
CVSS: 3.1 (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N)
CWE: CWE-209 (Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information)
OWASP: A05:2021 - Security Misconfiguration

### FINDING: Unauthenticated Access to User Identity Endpoint
SEVERITY: Low
EVIDENCE: `"url":"http://192.168.1.49:3000/rest/user/whoami","status_code":200,"content_length":11`
REASONING: The `/rest/user/whoami` endpoint returns a 200 OK status without authentication. While the content length is small (11 bytes), it confirms that the application allows unauthenticated queries to user identity endpoints, which may leak default user information or session state.
EXPLOITABILITY: Easy — Publicly accessible.
IMPACT: Information leakage, potential enumeration of default users or session behavior.
REMEDIATION: Ensure all `/rest/user/*` endpoints require valid authentication tokens.
CVSS: 3.1 (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N)
CWE: CWE-200 (Information Exposure)
OWASP: A01:2021 - Broken Access Control

SUMMARY: The target is identified as "OWASP Juice Shop," a deliberately vulnerable application. The scan confirms several known issues: an outdated jQuery version (High severity XSS risk), exposed admin configuration and metrics endpoints (Information Disclosure), and verbose error handling. While authentication is enforced on many API endpoints (401 responses), critical administrative and monitoring endpoints are accessible without credentials. The presence of specific error messages also indicates a lack of proper error handling.

### TOOL_HINTS

```json
      "http://192.168.1.49:3000/rest/track-order/:id"
    ],

  },
  "nuclei": {
    "extra_tags": ["xss", "cve", "exposure"],
    "priority_urls": [
      "http://192.168.1.49:3000/",
      "http://192.168.1.49:3000/rest/admin/application-configuration"
    ]
  },
  "rate_hint": 0.5
}
```

### REASONING

**Step 1 — Parse:**
I analyzed the ZAP output JSON.
- **Tooling Issues:** The `warnings` array indicates failures in the ZAP spider (`Failed to disable robots.txt handling`, `AJAX spider failed`). These are HTTP 400 errors against the ZAP API itself, not the target. They indicate a misconfiguration of the scanner or network connectivity to the scanner, but do not represent vulnerabilities in the target application.
- **Alerts:** I identified 12 alerts in the `alerts` array.
  - **Type A (CSP):** 6 alerts (IDs 0, 1, 2, 6, 10, and truncated) for "Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set" (Plugin 10038).
  - **Type B (CORS):** 5 alerts (IDs 3, 4, 5, 7, 9) for "Cross-Domain Misconfiguration" (Plugin 10098).
  - **Type C (3rd Party JS):** 2 alerts (IDs 8, and truncated) for "Cross-Domain JavaScript Source File Inclusion" (Plugin 10017).

**Step 2 — Pattern:**
- **CSP Missing:** Matches OWASP A05:2021 (Security Misconfiguration). Lack of CSP increases risk of XSS, but is a hardening issue, not an immediate exploit.
- **CORS `*`:** Matches OWASP A01:2021 (Broken Access Control). The evidence `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` allows any domain to read responses.
- **3rd Party JS:** Matches OWASP A08:2021 (Software and Data Integrity Failures). Loading scripts from CDNs introduces supply chain risks.

**Step 3 — Validate:**
- **CSP:** Evidence is the absence of the header (implied by the alert description). The tool explicitly flags this.
- **CORS:** Evidence is explicit: `"evidence": "Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *"`. The `other` field confirms the misconfiguration permits cross-domain reads.
- **3rd Party JS:** Evidence is explicit: `"evidence": "<script src=\"//cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/cookieconsent2/3.1.0/cookieconsent.min.js\"></script>"`.

**Step 4 — Assess (Exploitability):**
- **CSP:** Low exploitability. Requires a separate XSS vulnerability to be impactful.
- **CORS:** Moderate exploitability. If the API endpoints (`/api/Users`, `/api/Products/:id`, etc.) return sensitive data without authentication, an attacker can host a malicious page to steal that data via the browser. The ZAP output notes: "This misconfiguration could be used by an attacker to access data that is available in an unauthenticated manner".

misconfigurations.description: Running containers with 'root' user can lead to a container escape situation. It is a best practice to run containers as non-root users, which can be done by adding a 'USER' statement to the Dockerfile.

misconfigurations.resolution: Add 'USER <non root user name>' line to the Dockerfile

misconfigurations.title: 'apk add' is missing '--no-cache'
misconfigurations.description: You should use 'apk add' with '--no-cache' to clean package cached data and reduce image size.

findings.DetectorDescription: A JSON Web Token (JWT) is an approach to authentication or authorization that does not depend on server-side data. It may allow access to protected resources.
A JSON Web Token (JWT) is an approach to authentication or authorization that does not depend on server-side data. It may allow access to protected resources.
Private keys are used for securely connecting and authenticating to various systems and services. Exposure of private keys can lead to unauthorized access and data breaches.

leaks.Author: Josh Grossman
leaks.Message:
Update from upstream (#1)

* Remove reference of node.js-version specific Docker images

* Update REFERENCES.md

Added my talk at BSidesTLV

* Fixed broken label into LABEL_RECYCLE_QUANTITY

* Remove juice reference (#362)
and reformat markdown files

* Change donation/merchandise label for customized apps
(solves #362)

* Add video, code and scan results
(of @Soluto's integration with ZAP and Webdriver/Selenium)

* Add missing exclamation point

* Add more API backend tests
- app config
- password reset

* Remove unused config variable

* Add "Jawa Script T-Shirt" product

* Update test dependencies

* Fall back to Protractor 4

* Migrate first batch of API tests to frisby v2

* Migrate file serving API tests to frisby2

* Add X-XSS-Protection header absence test

* Migrate complaint API tests to frisby2

* Use actually existing Complaint resource

* Move HTTP request logging to top of router chain

* Adapt to frisby2 URI encoding behavior

* Adapt to JSON non-strict expectations
(see https://github.com/vlucas/frisby/issues/365)

* Set `application/json` content header for POST requests

* Migrate recycle API tests to frisby2

* Migrate feedback API tests to frisby2

* Migrate but disable file upload tests for frisby2
(see https://github.com/vlucas/frisby/issues/372)

findings.message: Possibility of prototype polluting function detected. By adding or modifying attributes of an object prototype, it is possible to create attributes that exist on every object, or replace critical attributes with malicious ones. This can be problematic if the software depends on existence or non-existence of certain attributes, or uses pre-defined attributes of object prototype (such as hasOwnProperty, toString or valueOf). Possible mitigations might be: freezing the object prototype, using an object without prototypes (via Object.create(null) ), blocking modifications of attributes that resolve to object prototype, using Map instead of object.
cwe: CWE-915: Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes

findings.message: This tag is missing an 'integrity' subresource integrity attribute. The 'integrity' attribute allows for the browser to verify that externally hosted files (for example from a CDN) are delivered without unexpected manipulation. Without this attribute, if an attacker can modify the externally hosted resource, this could lead to XSS and other types of attacks. To prevent this, include the base64-encoded cryptographic hash of the resource (file) you’re telling the browser to fetch in the 'integrity' attribute for all externally hosted files.
cwe: CWE-353: Missing Support for Integrity Check

findings.message: RegExp() called with a `challengeKey` function argument, this might allow an attacker to cause a Regular Expression Denial-of-Service (ReDoS) within your application as RegExP blocks the main thread. For this reason, it is recommended to use hardcoded regexes instead. If your regex is run on user-controlled input, consider performing input validation or use a regex checking/sanitization library such as https://www.npmjs.com/package/recheck to verify that the regex does not appear vulnerable to ReDoS.
cwe: CWE-1333: Inefficient Regular Expression Complexity
